title: Week of March 23, 2026
summary: This week brought major improvements to Salesforce automation reliability, with enhanced routing and validation that prevents errors before they occur. We also expanded our platform to support HubSpot and Marketo with the same execution tracking that ensures your revenue operations run smoothly. Additionally, Windows users now get a streamlined setup experience with native installation support.

entries.headline: Salesforce automations now run more reliably with better error prevention
Salesforce data sync now tracks all operations for complete visibility
details: All Salesforce workflows and processes are now monitored to ensure nothing gets missed
HubSpot and Marketo connections now use the same reliable tracking system

entries.headline: HubSpot and Marketo now get the same execution tracking as Salesforce
details: You can now see exactly what happened with your HubSpot and Marketo operations
All platforms now use the same reliable execution monitoring system

entries.headline: Salesforce process extraction now works more reliably
details: Flow and workflow discovery now uses more reliable methods
All automation branches are tracked for complete visibility

entries.headline: Salesforce automation inventory now captures everything
details: Apex triggers and classes are now tracked with full visibility
Flow harvesting includes complete branch tracking

entries.headline: Production data reads are now safer and more controlled
details: Read-only operations on production orgs now follow strict safety policies
Three-tier production access controls prevent accidental changes

entries.headline: Release notes are now generated automatically
details: Weekly release notes are created automatically from development activity
Updates are published automatically when new features are deployed

entries.headline: Fixed six issues with overly strict automation controls
details: Certain legitimate operations were being blocked unnecessarily
Added bypass options for verified safe operations
Permission management now works more smoothly

entries.headline: Simple record lookups now work without extra routing steps
details: Single record queries and small result sets bypass complex routing
Quick verification queries complete faster

entries.headline: Windows setup now installs Claude Code and Node.js automatically
details: Windows users get streamlined installation of required tools
Setup process now handles all dependencies natively on Windows

entries.headline: System now detects and reports connection issues automatically
details: Authentication problems are caught and reported clearly
Parent systems get notified when subsystems encounter issues
Salesforce org validation happens before operations start

entries.headline: Read-only Salesforce queries now work on production orgs
Revenue assessments now capture context for better recommendations
You can now customize templates and branding safely
details: Customizations persist through platform updates
Brand gallery and templates can be personalized without losing changes
Customizations are stored securely and backed up automatically

entries.headline: RevOps assessments now find your revenue fields and sales processes automatically
details: System detects custom revenue fields like Units, Net New ARR, and ACV
Sales process analysis adapts to your org's specific setup
All revenue field references are now personalized to your configuration

entries.headline: Setup now installs required dependencies automatically
Windows users get a dedicated PowerShell setup script
Setup experience is now smoother with better detection
New tools prevent problematic Salesforce deployments
details: Picklist and record type conflicts are caught before deployment
Field history limits are validated to prevent data loss
Configuration merging prevents duplicate settings errors

entries.headline: Added automatic secret scanning to prevent credential leaks
Enhanced security scanning with automated credential detection
Removed hardcoded credentials for better security
